questions.question: Which is the largest continent in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent covering about 44.6 million km², which is about 30% of Earth's total land area.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: How many states does India have as of 2024?
questions.explanation: India has 28 states and 8 Union Territories as of 2024, after Jammu & Kashmir was reorganized into 2 UTs in 2019.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, at an average distance of about 57.9 million km.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: The national fruit of India is:
questions.explanation: Mango (Mangifera indica) is the national fruit of India. It is known as the 'King of Fruits'.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which year did India gain independence?
questions.explanation: India gained independence from British rule on 15 August 1947.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: What is the capital of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is the capital of Rajasthan. It is known as the Pink City due to the dominant color of its buildings.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Iron Man of India'?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is known as the Iron Man of India for his role in uniting the princely states after independence.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Which organ pumps blood throughout the body?
questions.explanation: The heart is the muscular organ that pumps blood through the circulatory system to all parts of the body.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: In which year was the Indian National Congress founded?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 by Allan Octavian Hume, Dadabhai Naoroji, and Dinshaw Wacha.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Which is the national bird of India?
questions.explanation: The Indian Peacock (Pavo cristatus) is the national bird of India, declared so in 1963.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What does CPU stand for?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit. It is the primary component of a computer that performs instructions.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which is the smallest state of India by area?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest state of India by area, covering about 3,702 km².
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: The Statue of Unity is dedicated to which leader?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity in Gujarat is dedicated to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. It is the world's tallest statue at 182 meters.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb Carbon Dioxide (CO₂) from the atmosphere during photosynthesis and release oxygen.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: Which is the highest mountain peak in India?
questions.explanation: Kangchenjunga at 8,586 m is the highest mountain peak entirely in India (on the border of Sikkim and Nepal).
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: The Indian Constitution came into effect on:
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India came into effect on 26 January 1950, which is celebrated as Republic Day.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing the first practical telephone in 1876.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
questions.explanation: The Kosi River is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar' because of its frequent floods and the havoc it causes.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: What is the SI unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: Ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current. It is named after French physicist André-Marie Ampère.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: India's first satellite was named:
questions.explanation: Aryabhata was India's first satellite, launched on 19 April 1975. It was named after the ancient Indian mathematician.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Who wrote the Indian National Anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'?
questions.explanation: Jana Gana Mana was written by Rabindranath Tagore. It was adopted as the national anthem on 24 January 1950.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following is known as the 'Queen of Spices'?
questions.explanation: Cardamom is known as the 'Queen of Spices'. Black Pepper is known as the 'King of Spices'.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The Sundarbans mangrove forest is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans is the world's largest mangrove forest, located in the delta of the Ganges in West Bengal (and Bangladesh).
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Which vitamin deficiency causes night blindness?
questions.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency causes night blindness (nyctalopia). Vitamin A is essential for the production of rhodopsin in the retina.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: Who is the author of 'Wings of Fire'?
questions.explanation: Wings of Fire is an autobiography of A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, co-written with Arun Tiwari. Published in 1999.
questions.subject: General Awareness
